data.body:
PASS 1 (A1 anti-slop/over-engineering/NIH): CLEAN — no findings.

Verified against the codebase: this is a maintenance sweep that introduces zero new abstractions, dependencies, or config keys. It reuses three established in-repo patterns rather than rebuilding anything:
- `# noqa: BLE001` justification convention — already the documented contract (`src/rebar/_logging.py:36`) and used pervasively (plan_review/*, _engine/*).
- `logging.getLogger(__name__)` named-logger migration — the documented stderr-diagnostic target (`_logging.py:48`).
- The shrinking per-file-ignores allowlist strangler (`pyproject.toml:186-231`); the targeted entries exist exactly as the plan states (`grounding/*`, `grounding/detectors/*`, `_engine_support/*`).

The BLE0001 landmine is real: `src/rebar/grounding/harness.py:281` carries `# noqa: BLE0001` (extra 0) on an `except BaseException`, so the noqa is inert — accurate. No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization / premature-abstraction / config-proliferation patterns detected.
